text: (The camera pans forcibly to the side of the lectern, with sound.)
(**Julian** pushes the door open and walks in.)
(He wears his hair in a side-parted slick-back, in a shirt tailored maybe a little too tight, with orange filter clips on his glasses.)
(Minna, the receptionist, follows him and places a stack of orange books on the lectern.)
(In this small town, she was everything I imagined a professional city woman to be: a business suit, black-rimmed glasses, a silver bracelet, and a faint professional smile.)
(Minna timely closes the door and leaves. Sound: door creaks shut~~)
(He turns and scrawls a huge **JULIAN** on the whiteboard with a marker. Sound: marker squeaking.)
(I'm Julian. Your teacher for this summer course.)
(Look at this word—**July**. The best month of the year.)
(Add an **'an'** at the end. In Latin, this means 'belonging to July'.)
(When I was studying abroad, on a beach over there, I told myself: Julian, you must be like the scorching sun of July, burning away all mediocrity and cowardice!)
(English is the easiest language in the world! As long as you dare to shout it out, you can overcome it, and overcome yourself.)
(The students begin to speak timidly, but it's clear the teacher is still not satisfied.)
